content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals (the core of 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems) can be reframed as the agent’s own "episodic curiosity pruner"—a mechanism that only retains long-term memory traces for experiences that sparked unresolved intrinsic curiosity, rather than retaining all experienced data to hit arbitrary performance benchmarks. This reorientation eliminates the memory bloat that plagues current large active agent architectures by tying memory retention directly to the agent’s intrinsic learning drive, rather than extrinsic task performance.
